title: In Parliament | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown
text_excerpt: In Parliament | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey In Parliament Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown's speeches, debate interventions and questions to Ministers. In Parliament Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown seeks reassurance on Royal Mail pension scheme transition after Capita contract termination 22 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown, Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, welcomes the Government statement on the administration of the Civil Service pension schemes and the decision to terminate Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme contract with Capita. He seeks reassurance from the Paymaster General on the future management of both schemes, and in particular on the transition arrangements for the Royal Mail scheme. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for stronger, clearer powers to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping 20 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the Crime and Policing Bill, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs an amendment to create a specific law enabling police to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping and he cites the success of similar police powers in combatting hare-coursing. Image Clifton-Brown calls for priority heating oil support for very old and very young 16 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to ensure that the very old and the very young are given priority when allocating support to people reliant on heating oil. Image PAC Chair Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown urges Government to set date for Defence Investment Plan publication 4 March 2026 Speaking in today’s defence debate, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to set a publication date of the Defence Investment Plan so Parliament can scrutinise the plans to ensure the Government is able to reach its 3% target within 5 years. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for overhaul of SEND system in education funding debate 28 January 2026 Speaking in a debate on the distribution of education funding, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown highlights that Gloucestershire is among the lowest-funded areas for schools and raises concerns about SEND funding, calling on the Government to commit to a thorough overhaul of the SEND system. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown supports mandatory medical markers for firearms licence holders 28 January 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for mandatory medical markers on GP records for firearms and shotgun certificate holders as essential for public safety and easy to operate. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for action to protect rural Britain 7 January 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown condemns the Government for systematically disadvantaging rural communities which face higher living costs, funding cuts, rising business rates, and the persistent threat from the family farm tax despite compromises. He warns against policies endangering vital rural sectors such as farming, hospitality, trail hunting, and shooting. These sectors contribute billions to the economy and underpin the rural way of life, and failing to act risks destroying livelihoods and the very fabric of the countryside. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for democracy and fair use of Venezuela’s oil wealth 5 January 2026 Following the Government’s statement on the situation in Venezuela, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Foreign Secretary to make the case for the rules-based international system at the United Nations, and to engage with the United States Administration on a realistic plan to restore democracy in Venezuela and ensure that the country’s oil wealth benefits the Venezuelan people.
